content: read reviews/291.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/291.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/291.md.

    32→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    33→- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase` (`lcmUpTo`, `harmonicLcmNumerator`).
    34→- [x] No local notation used (none needed).

    36→

    38→

    40→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_291` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    41→- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.

    43→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring referencing `[ErGr80, p.34]`.
    44→- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the second (solved) part.
    45→
    46→### `answer()` usage
    47→
    48→The original problem asks "Is it true that … both occur for infinitely many n?" — this is a **yes/no question (Case 1)**.
    49→
    50→- [x] Main theorem uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form (correct for Case 1).
    51→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: both `gcd = 1` and `gcd > 1` occur for infinitely many `n`.
    52→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ N, ∃ n, ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    53→- [x] Variant `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` is a direct assertion (Case 3) — correctly uses no `answer()` wrapper.
    54→
    55→## 7. Category Attribute
    56→
    57→- [x] `erdos_291` has exactly one `@[category research open]`.
    58→- [x] `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` has exactly one `@[category research solved]`.

    60→- [x] The `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    61→
    62→## 8. AMS Attribute
    63→
    64→- [x] Both theorems have exactly one `@[AMS 11]` attribute.
    65→- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
    66→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number Theory).
    67→- [x] Ordering/duplicates: N/A (single code).

    72→- [x] `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` uses `by\n  sorry` — acceptable.
    73→- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    74→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    75→- [x] No `native_decide`.

    82→- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the second (trivially solved) part, citing Steinerberger.
    83→

    88→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/291.lean` — Erdős Problem 291

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 291`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/291`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (LCM/harmonic-sum gcd question).
- [x] Bibliography entry for `[ErGr80]` included.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos291 ... end Erdos291`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos291`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `lcmUpTo` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `harmonicLcmNumerator` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — `Finset.lcm`, `Finset.sum`, and natural-number division are computable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase` (`lcmUpTo`, `harmonicLcmNumerator`).
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. **(Minor — checklist says "consider", not required.)**

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both `erdos_291` and `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_291` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring referencing `[ErGr80, p.34]`.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the second (solved) part.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that … both occur for infinitely many n?" — this is a **yes/no question (Case 1)**.

- [x] Main theorem uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form (correct for Case 1).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: both `gcd = 1` and `gcd > 1` occur for infinitely many `n`.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ N, ∃ n, ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
- [x] Variant `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` is a direct assertion (Case 3) — correctly uses no `answer()` wrapper.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] `erdos_291` has exactly one `@[category research open]`.
- [x] `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` has exactly one `@[category research solved]`.
- [x] Both values are valid categories.
- [x] The `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Both theorems have exactly one `@[AMS 11]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number Theory).
- [x] Ordering/duplicates: N/A (single code).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] `erdos_291` uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` — acceptable.
- [x] `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` uses `by\n  sorry` — acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_291.variants.gcd_gt_one` (follows `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 11]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the second (trivially solved) part, citing Steinerberger.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `≤`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — **not verified** (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linters pass — **not verified** (requires build environment).

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (pending build verification)**

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. The problem is correctly identified as a yes/no question (Case 1) and uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` accordingly. The variant for the solved second part is properly separated with `category research solved` and no `answer()` wrapper. Definitions are well-documented, properly named, and computable. The only non-checked items are the optional sanity-check attributes on definitions and build verification (which requires the build environment).

content.text: The review status is **PASS (pending build verification)**. All checklist items passed (the unchecked items are optional sanity-check attributes and build verification which requires the build environment). No changes needed.
